key_insight: A self-supervised, label-agnostic pipeline can discover clinically meaningful tibial plateau fracture phenotypes directly from radiographs, bypassing inconsistent human labels.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more robust fracture characterization workflow for computer-aided diagnosis, especially where expert classification systems show high inter-observer variability.
method: RadImageNet-pretrained ResNet-50 was fine-tuned with SimCLR on 154 cleaned knee radiographs.
UMAP and k-means were used to derive four imaging-based phenotypes.
Two blinded clinicians reviewed the cluster outputs for phenotype coherence and clinical plausibility.
evidence_signal: The four clusters showed moderate stability and strong cohesion (bootstrap ARI 0.319±0.041, silhouette 0.511), while agreement with Schatzker labels was near zero (ARI 0.013).
watch_item: The study uses a relatively small single-dataset sample, so external validation across scanners, sites, and fracture distributions is still needed.
paper_title: Phenotyping TPF via Self-Supervised Learning: A Label-Agnostic Framework with Expert Validation
